name: Narasimha

visual_attributes.morphology: Human torso with a lion's head and claws
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Larger than a human, imposing and majestic
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Human-like body with lion features integrated seamlessly

domain_specific_visual.ICONOGRAPHY: Depicted in both fierce (ugra) and serene (saumya) forms
domain_specific_visual.THIRD_EYE: Possesses a third eye similar to Shiva

functional_attributes.primary_function: Protector of devotees and destroyer of evil

domain_specific_functional.AVATAR_ROLE: Circumvented the boon of Hiranyakashipu to protect Prahlada
domain_specific_functional.SYMBOLISM: Represents the triumph of good over evil

relationships.description: Narasimha killed Hiranyakashipu to protect his devotee Prahlada
Protected Prahlada from his father Hiranyakashipu
Narasimha is the fourth avatar of Vishnu

contextual_attributes.historical_period: Referenced from Vedic times to early centuries CE
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Central figure in Vaishnava theology and iconography

contrastive_constraints: NOT a human without lion features
NOT a generic deity without specific iconographic elements
